originalTitle: At least 100 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, official says
title: Big Hearts Help Stop Sickness in Congo!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here. Today, we have something important to talk about. It’s about a place called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a country in our big, beautiful continent of Africa. Sometimes, people there get a sickness called Ebola. And brave doctors and helpers are working very, very hard to make everyone safe and healthy again.

It's okay to feel a little bit curious or even a little bit worried when you hear about sickness. But remember, we are going to learn together. We will understand what is happening and how grown-ups are helping. Learning makes us strong and smart!

What is This Sickness Called Ebola?

Ebola is a kind of sickness. It is caused by a tiny, tiny germ. You cannot see these germs with your eyes, just like you can't see the air! These germs are so small, you need a special microscope to see them. When these germs get into a person's body, they can make them very, very sick.

Did you know? Ebola is NOT like a regular cold or flu. It's much less common, which means fewer people get it. And it doesn't spread through the air when someone coughs or sneezes, like a cold does. This is important to remember.

Doctors and scientists have studied Ebola for many years. They know how it acts and how to fight it. It's a tough sickness, but doctors are tougher!

Where is DR Congo?

DR Congo is a really big country right in the middle of Africa. If you look at a map of Africa, you will see it. It has many green forests and big rivers. Many wonderful people live there, just like you!

Sometimes, Ebola sickness happens in different parts of Africa. But right now, we are talking about our friends in DR Congo. They are strong, and they have many helpers from all over the world.

Did you know? The Democratic Republic of Congo is the second-largest country in Africa! It has amazing wildlife and beautiful places.

How Does Ebola Make People Sick?

When someone gets Ebola, they start to feel very unwell. They might get a high fever, which means their body feels very hot. They might also get a big tummy ache and feel very tired, like they don't have any energy to play or even get out of bed.

Some people might also feel like throwing up or have a headache. It's like having a very, very bad flu. That's why it's so important for doctors to help people quickly when they start to feel sick.

Doctors and nurses are super brave. They take care of people who are sick with Ebola. They give them medicines and make sure they are comfortable. They are like superheroes in special protective suits!

How Does Ebola Spread?

This is a very important part! Ebola does NOT spread easily. It doesn't spread through the air, like when someone sneezes. You cannot catch it by just being near someone who is sick, or by sharing food or toys in a regular way.

Ebola spreads when you touch the body fluids of someone who is very sick with Ebola. What are body fluids? Things like blood, or sweat, or vomit, or other things that come out of a sick person's body. That's why doctors wear special suits, gloves, and masks. These special clothes keep them safe when they help sick people.

Did you know? Bats and some monkeys can carry the Ebola germ. But people usually get it from other people who are sick, not directly from animals in everyday life. It's very rare!

This is why washing your hands is super important! If you wash your hands often with soap and water, you are helping to keep yourself safe from many kinds of germs, not just Ebola germs. It’s a super power you have!

What Are Grown-Ups Doing to Help?

Many, many grown-ups are helping in DR Congo. There are doctors, nurses, scientists, and special helpers from many countries. One very important group helping is called the World Health Organization, or WHO for short. Can you say "WHO"? It's like saying "who is helping?" and the answer is WHO!

WHO is like a team of super doctors and health experts from all over the world. They help countries when there are big sicknesses. They send doctors, nurses, medicines, and advice. They teach people how to stay safe and stop the sickness from spreading.

They are working hard to find people who are sick and give them help. They are also teaching everyone about how to wash hands and be careful. They want to make sure everyone in DR Congo is safe.

Did you know? Scientists have made special medicines and even vaccines to help fight Ebola. A vaccine is like a special shield that helps your body fight off germs before you get sick!

What is an 'Emergency'?

Sometimes, when a sickness like Ebola gets very big, the World Health Organization (WHO) says it's an "international emergency." What does that mean?

It means that many, many people are getting sick, and it's a big problem. When WHO says it's an "international emergency," it's like a big call for help to all the countries in the world. It means, "Hey everyone, we need to work together even more to stop this sickness!"

So, when WHO said this about Ebola in DR Congo, it meant that more help, more doctors, more medicines, and more money would come from all over the world to help our friends in DR Congo. It's a way to make sure that everyone acts fast and helps as much as they can.

It's not to scare us, but to tell us that many people care and are coming to help. It’s like when your parents call for help from neighbors if something big happens – everyone comes together!

How Can We Stay Safe and Help?

You, as a super kid, can help too! Even if you are far away from DR Congo, you can do many things to stay healthy and safe from all kinds of germs:

  1. Wash Your Hands! This is the BEST thing you can do. Wash your hands with soap and clean water many times a day. Wash before you eat, after you play, after you use the toilet, and whenever your hands look dirty. Sing a song while you wash for 20 seconds!
  2. Don't Touch Your Face! Try not to touch your eyes, nose, or mouth with your hands. Germs love to sneak in that way.
  3. Eat Healthy Food! Eating yummy fruits and vegetables helps your body stay strong and fight off germs.
  4. Get Enough Sleep! Your body needs rest to be strong.
  5. Listen to Grown-Ups! Always listen to your parents, teachers, and health workers. They know what is best to keep you safe and healthy.

By doing these things, you are not just helping yourself, but you are also helping your family and your community stay healthy. You are a health hero!

Remember to Be Brave!

It's true that Ebola is a serious sickness. But remember all the brave people helping in DR Congo. Doctors, nurses, and scientists are working tirelessly to stop it. They are finding new ways to treat people and keep everyone safe.

We send our love and strength to our friends in DR Congo. And we know that by learning about germs, washing our hands, and listening to grown-ups, we can all stay healthy and safe. You are smart, you are strong, and you can make a difference!

Keep shining brightly, young African reporters!
summary: Brave doctors and helpers, with help from the World Health Organization, are working hard to stop a serious sickness called Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, teaching everyone how to stay safe by washing hands and listening to grown-ups.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how important it is for everyone, especially grown-ups and special health teams, to work together quickly to help people who are sick and keep our communities safe from big sicknesses.

title: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
content: An outbreak of Ebola in DR Congo involves a rare strain and is in an area affected by conflict.

originalTitle: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
title: Good News! Leaders Choose Peace Over Fighting!
content:
Jambo, my wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter from sunny Africa, here to share some very important and happy news with you all today! We have some big news about two countries far, far away, and it's all about choosing peace instead of fighting. Isn't that wonderful?

Big News From Far Away Lands

Sometimes, grown-ups in charge of countries have big disagreements. It's a bit like when you and your friend might disagree over which game to play, but on a much, much bigger scale! When countries disagree, sometimes people worry that they might start fighting. Fighting can be very scary and sad because it can hurt people and break things. But today, we have good news! A very important leader, Mr. Donald Trump, who is the President of a country called the United States of America (we can just call it the US for short), had a plan. He was thinking about sending his country's army to another country called Iran. But guess what? He stopped! He said, "No fighting today!" Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in the world! Each country has its own special name, its own flag, and its own leader, just like your school has a head teacher!

Who is Mr. Trump?

Mr. Donald Trump is like the main leader of the United States. Think of him as the head of a very big family, or the captain of a very big team. His job is to help his country and its people. Sometimes, leaders have to make very big and difficult decisions. Mr. Trump makes decisions for the US, which is a big country far away from Africa. Did you know? The United States of America is a very big country, and many different kinds of people live there! It's famous for things like big cities and tall buildings.

What About Iran?

Iran is another country, also very far away. It's a land with its own people, its own history, and its own beautiful culture. Just like every country, Iran has its own government and leaders too. Sometimes, countries don't always agree on everything, and that's what was happening between the US and Iran. Did you know? Iran is in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has a very long history, with ancient stories and amazing old buildings!

Why Was There a Plan to Fight?

Well, without going into all the grown-up details, the US and Iran were having some really big problems and disagreements. These problems made some leaders in the US think about using their army, which we call an "attack" or "fighting." But fighting is never a good thing. It makes people sad, it can hurt homes and families, and it stops people from living peacefully. Imagine if you and your friend had a big disagreement and instead of talking, you started pushing and shoving. That wouldn't be nice, would it? It's the same for countries, but much bigger and scarier. Did you know? Most people in the world want to live in peace, without any fighting! We all want to feel safe and happy in our homes.

Why Did Mr. Trump Stop the Plan?

This is the really good news! Mr. Trump said he called off, or stopped, the attack because some other countries asked him to. These countries are called "Gulf states." They are like friends or neighbours to Iran, and they live near a big ocean called the Persian Gulf. They knew that fighting would be bad for everyone, including them. So, these Gulf states, like good friends, went to Mr. Trump and said, "Please, Mr. President, let's not fight. Let's try to talk instead." They asked him to think about peace and to try to find a way to solve the problems without any fighting. Did you know? Asking for help and talking about problems is a very brave and smart thing to do, even for big leaders! It helps avoid bigger problems.

Talking is Better Than Fighting!

And guess what Mr. Trump said? He listened to his friends, the Gulf states! He said, "Serious talks are now happening." This means that instead of fighting, the leaders of these countries are going to sit down and talk to each other. They will try to understand each other's problems and find solutions without anyone getting hurt. This is called "negotiations," which is just a big word for talking to solve a problem. It's like when you and your friend talk about sharing a toy so you both can play happily. Talking can make things much, much better than fighting ever could. Did you know? Talking things through is usually the best way to solve disagreements, whether it's between two friends or two big countries!

Why is This Good News for Everyone?

This is such great news because it means less sadness, less fear, and more hope for peace in the world. When countries choose to talk instead of fight, it shows that they care about people's lives and about making the world a safer place for everyone, including you and me! It reminds us that even when things are difficult, there is always a chance for peace. It shows that leaders can listen to good advice and choose the path of talking and understanding. That's a very important lesson for all of us. From your friends in Africa, we always hope for peace and kindness everywhere in the world. So, let's all be happy today that leaders are choosing to talk and work things out peacefully. Hip hip hooray for peace!
summary: The President of the United States, Mr. Trump, stopped a plan to attack Iran because other countries asked him to, and now they are all talking to solve their problems peacefully.
whyMatters: This is important because choosing to talk instead of fight means fewer people will get hurt and the world can be a safer, happier place for everyone.
